title: Claude Code Superpowers — Skills Palette + Subagent Visual Upgrade + Context Window Meter
overview: Ship three first-class desktop surfaces for Claude Code mechanics that today live invisibly in the terminal: a Cmd+K Skills Palette that fuzzy-searches .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md and invokes them inline, a richer Subagent visualizer with nested-tree rendering and role-colored cards with elapsed-time clocks and click-to-focus, and a Context Window Meter promoted into the AppHeader with an extended tooltip showing token breakdown, cost, and an auto-compact estimate.
scopeChallenge: Significant scaffolding already exists. ContextRing component (src/renderer/components/chat/ContextRing.tsx) is built but rendered only inside ChatInput; relocation + tooltip extension is the work, not a build. AcpSubagentDisplay is a 182-line component with expand/collapse, status icons, role labels, and progress bar; the work is enhancement (nested tree, role color, elapsed clock, click-to-focus), not rewrite. Skills are parsed by claude_config.rs::scan_skills but ClaudeSkill lacks description and bodyExcerpt — parser extension plus a dedicated palette modal. Fuzzy search algorithm (lib/fuzzy-search.ts) and Radix Dialog primitive (components/ui/dialog.tsx) are reused as-is. Pre-existing plans/claude-code-features.md already shipped permissions, output styles, hooks viewer, statusline, slash-command browser, and CLAUDE.md memory editor — this plan deliberately avoids overlap. EXPANSION mode selected because three distinct features each have nontrivial edge cases that deserve dedicated test tasks plus polish and documentation tasks.

failureModes.mitigation: Audit useKeyboardShortcuts.ts first; if claimed, fall back to Cmd+Shift+K and document in TASK-019

failureModes.risk: Two ContextRing instances drift (header vs chat input)
failureModes.mitigation: Both read from same useTaskStore.tasks[id].usage source. Snapshot test in TASK-017 verifies parity

failureModes.risk: Skills with no description leave palette cards looking empty
failureModes.mitigation: Fall back to bodyExcerpt; if both missing, fall back to filePath. Truncate via Tailwind line-clamp-2

failureModes.risk: Skill body could be huge — palette UI explodes
failureModes.mitigation: Body excerpt is server-side truncated by body_excerpt Rust helper; palette card additionally clamps via Tailwind line-clamp-2

failureModes.risk: ACP payload does not yet expose parent field on subagents
failureModes.mitigation: Tree builder falls back to flat list when no agent has parent — preserves today's behavior

failureModes.risk: Circular parent references in subagent payload
failureModes.mitigation: Detect cycle via visited-set during tree build; emit console.warn and render flat

failureModes.risk: splash-insert CustomEvent not heard when no input is mounted
failureModes.mitigation: Hook copies skill body to clipboard as fallback and surfaces a warning toast

failureModes.risk: Elapsed clock leaks setInterval after card unmounts
failureModes.mitigation: Cleanup in useEffect return; verify in TASK-018 with vi.useFakeTimers()

failureModes.risk: Tooltip exceeds Radix max-width and clips
failureModes.mitigation: Apply className max-w-[280px] to TooltipContent; switch to Popover if rich content is needed

failureModes.risk: Auto-collapse fires before user can click an agent
failureModes.mitigation: Set userToggledRef.current = true on first card interaction

failureModes.risk: Description field with non-string YAML crashes Rust parser
failureModes.mitigation: Use serde_yaml::from_str into HashMap<String, Value> then coerce; non-string returns None

failureModes.risk: bun test (native runner) executes Vitest files
failureModes.mitigation: Verify bunfig.toml still redirects bun test to .bun-test-noop before merging

failureModes.risk: User-installed skill name collides with built-in slash command (e.g., a skill named 'model' shadows /model)
failureModes.mitigation: Detect collision at palette render time by intersecting config.skills[].name with SlashCommand[] from useSettingsStore. Show 'conflicts with built-in /name' badge on the card. Invocation still dispatches splash-insert with /skill-name; existing slash-command handler resolves built-ins first by design.

failureModes.risk: task.contextUsage.turns does not exist — earlier plan iteration referenced it
failureModes.mitigation: Derive avgPctPerTurn from Math.floor(task.messages.length / 2) (assistant-message count proxy) with fallback of 5 when message count is zero. task.contextUsage at src/renderer/types/index.ts:120 carries no turn count.

tasks.title: Extend ClaudeSkill TS interface with description and bodyExcerpt fields
tasks.description: Add two optional fields to ClaudeSkill in src/renderer/types/index.ts:325 — description?: string parsed from SKILL.md frontmatter, and bodyExcerpt?: string from the first ~200 chars after frontmatter. Fields flow through serde camelCase from Rust. No Rust changes here; this task only ensures the TypeScript surface accepts the fields when TASK-003 emits them.
acceptanceCriteria: ClaudeSkill has description?: string and bodyExcerpt?: string optional fields
All existing consumers (ClaudeSkillRow.tsx, claudeConfigStore.ts) compile without changes
bun run check:ts passes (failure case: extra fields must not break existing tests)

tasks.title: Create subagent role-to-style mapping module
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/lib/subagent-style.ts exporting getSubagentRoleColor(role) returning { from, to, bg, text } and getSubagentRoleIcon(role) returning a Tabler React component. Role taxonomy: default | plan | guide | research | builder plus kiro_* aliases from AcpSubagentDisplay.tsx:11-19. Pure module, no React hooks. Icons from @tabler/icons-react per CLAUDE.md rule (no lucide-react).
acceptanceCriteria: Module exports two pure functions plus role taxonomy constant
Each known role returns distinct colors (no two roles share the same from gradient stop)
Unknown roles fall back to default without throwing (failure case: getSubagentRoleColor('garbage') returns default tier)

tasks.title: Extend Rust ClaudeSkill struct and scan_skills to parse description and body excerpt
tasks.description: Modify src-tauri/src/commands/claude_config.rs. Add description: Option<String> and body_excerpt: Option<String> to ClaudeSkill struct (line 24) keeping serde camelCase. In scan_skills (line 251), after locating each SKILL.md, call split_frontmatter (line 102) and body_excerpt helper (line 128) to populate fields. Missing description key yields None.
acceptanceCriteria: scan_skills populates description from YAML frontmatter description key
body_excerpt is truncated to ~200 chars and excludes heading lines
Skills with malformed YAML frontmatter return ClaudeSkill with None description rather than panicking (failure case)
bun run check:rust and bun run test:rust both pass

tasks.title: Create skillsPaletteStore Zustand store
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/stores/skillsPaletteStore.ts exporting useSkillsPaletteStore with state { isOpen, query, selectedIndex } and actions open, close, toggle, setQuery, setSelectedIndex, moveSelection. Apply bail-out guard pattern from CLAUDE.md (check value changed before set). setQuery resets selectedIndex to 0 on each query change.
acceptanceCriteria: All actions update state via bail-out-guarded set calls
setQuery resets selectedIndex to 0 on each query change
moveSelection clamps to [0, listLength-1] given list-length parameter (failure case: must not go negative or overflow)

tasks.title: Create useSkillInvoke hook to dispatch skill activation
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/hooks/useSkillInvoke.ts exporting useSkillInvoke() that returns invoke(skill: ClaudeSkill). Dispatches splash-insert CustomEvent on document with detail=/${skill.name} (same protocol as EmptyThreadSplash.tsx:13). Fallback if no active task: copy skill.bodyExcerpt ?? skill.name to navigator.clipboard with toast/warn. Always closes palette via useSkillsPaletteStore.getState().close().
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatches splash-insert with the correct slash-prefixed skill name
Closes the palette after invocation
When document is in an unusual state (no active input listener), invocation does not throw — only warns (failure case)

tasks.title: Add Cmd+K (Ctrl+K on non-mac) binding to toggle Skills Palette
tasks.description: Extend src/renderer/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts handler at line 38-40. Detect (metaKey || ctrlKey) && key === 'k' && !shiftKey && !altKey, call useSkillsPaletteStore.getState().toggle(), preventDefault. Allow Cmd+K to fire even from textarea (palette is top-level). Audit current bindings; if Cmd+K is claimed, switch to Cmd+Shift+K and document in TASK-019.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+K (Ctrl+K on Linux/Windows) toggles isOpen in useSkillsPaletteStore
Shortcut fires even when chat input is focused
Existing shortcuts (Cmd+L focus, Cmd+Shift+L split, Cmd+Shift+Y yolo) still work unchanged (failure case: regression test all three)

tasks.title: Build SkillsPalette modal component
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/components/chat/SkillsPalette.tsx — Radix Dialog-based palette. Reads useSkillsPaletteStore and useClaudeConfigStore((s) => s.config.skills). Filters via fuzzyScore(query, skill.name + ' ' + (skill.description ?? '')), sorts ascending, slices top 50. Renders fuzzy search input with ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape handlers. Each skill card: name, source badge (global/local), description (or bodyExcerpt fallback truncated 150 chars), filePath subtitle. Selected card has bg-accent highlight; click or Enter invokes useSkillInvoke. Empty state hints to install skills under ~/.claude/skills/. Match Dialog shell pattern from WhatsNewDialog.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: Fuzzy filter ranks exact-prefix matches highest (e.g., 'com' surfaces commit above cost-estimate)
ArrowDown beyond list length stays clamped to last item
Pressing Enter on an empty list does NOT crash and does NOT close palette (failure case)
Pressing Escape closes the palette without invoking any skill

tasks.title: Mount SkillsPalette as a global overlay in App.tsx
tasks.description: Mount <SkillsPalette /> once in src/renderer/App.tsx, outside the main layout flex so it does not affect layout when closed. Verify the keyboard hook from TASK-006 is active at the App level (it already is).
acceptanceCriteria: SkillsPalette is mounted exactly once in the React tree
Closed palette adds zero DOM cost (Radix Dialog uses a portal)
Mounting works whether the user is on Dashboard, ChatPanel, or SettingsPanel views (failure case: verify no view crashes when palette open)

tasks.title: Render ContextRing inside HeaderToolbar
tasks.description: Extend src/renderer/components/header-toolbar.tsx. Read useTaskStore for active task's usage.contextUsage and compactionStatus and render <ContextRing used={ctx.used} size={ctx.size} compactionStatus={status} /> when contextUsage exists. The existing ContextRing at src/renderer/components/chat/ContextRing.tsx is reused as-is. The duplicate inside ChatInput.tsx:129 stays; both read the same store value so they always agree.
acceptanceCriteria: AppHeader shows ContextRing when a task with usage is active
No ring shown when no task is active or contextUsage is null
Header ring and ChatInput ring always show same percentage (failure case: snapshot both, assert equality)

tasks.title: Extend ContextRing tooltip with token breakdown and auto-compact estimate
tasks.description: Modify src/renderer/components/chat/ContextRing.tsx. Replace single-line tooltipText (line 39-43) with structured tooltip body: row 1 total context %, row 2 input/output/cache-read/cache-creation token counts via formatTokens from UsagePanel.tsx:8, row 3 task cost via formatCost from UsagePanel.tsx:15, row 4 'Auto-compact in ~N turns' estimate computed as max(0, floor((100-pct)/avgPctPerTurn)) where avgPctPerTurn = pct / max(1, floor(task.messages.length / 2)) with fallback of 5 when message count is zero. task.contextUsage lives at src/renderer/types/index.ts:120 and has no turns field — derivation comes from message history. Apply max-w-[280px] to TooltipContent; switch to Popover if width insufficient.
acceptanceCriteria: Tooltip displays four labelled rows when full usage data is present
When per-category breakdown is missing (older task), tooltip gracefully shows only totals (failure case)
Auto-compact estimate is 0 (rendered as 'soon') when pct >= 95

tasks.title: Refactor AcpSubagentDisplay to nested-tree renderer
tasks.description: Modify src/renderer/components/chat/AcpSubagentDisplay.tsx. Replace flat subagents.map at line 119 with tree-builder that groups by optional parent field (check task-store-listeners.ts::parseSubagents at line 122 for payload shape). Each level applies paddingLeft: depth * 12px and a tree-line guide. If ACP payload lacks parent field, add parent?: string to SubagentInfo in types/index.ts:378 and parse it in parseSubagents. Fall back to flat list when no agent has a parent. Detect cycles via visited-set.
acceptanceCriteria: Subagents with parent field render indented under their parent card
When no subagent has a parent field, layout is identical to today (regression preserved)
Circular parent references do not cause infinite recursion (failure case: detect and render flat with a warning)

tasks.title: Enrich AgentCard with role color, elapsed-time clock, and depth indent
tasks.description: Modify AgentCard inside src/renderer/components/chat/AcpSubagentDisplay.tsx:133. Replace static IconRobot at line 144 with getSubagentRoleIcon(agent.role) from TASK-002. Tint the role badge using getSubagentRoleColor(agent.role).bg. Add elapsed-time clock next to status: track startedAt from first running transition, render MM:SS via setInterval (cleanup in useEffect return). Stop ticking when status is completed/failed. Accept depth prop for indent.
acceptanceCriteria: Each agent card shows role-specific icon and tinted role badge
Elapsed timer ticks once per second while status === 'running' and stops on terminal states
An agent that never transitions to running shows no clock at all (failure case: no '00:00' placeholder)

tasks.title: Add click-to-focus interaction to subagent cards
tasks.description: Modify both AcpSubagentDisplay.tsx and MessageList.tsx. No exported scroll helper exists today — MessageList only uses internal virtualizer.scrollToIndex at lines 175 and 259. (1) In MessageList.tsx, add a useEffect listener for the chat-scroll-to CustomEvent on document carrying detail: { messageId: string }. On receipt, locate the timeline row index for that messageId and call virtualizer.scrollToIndex(idx, { align: 'center', behavior: 'smooth' }); clean up listener on unmount. (2) In AcpSubagentDisplay.tsx, make each AgentCard clickable: find first tool call in active task's messages where tool_use_id or subagent_name matches agent.name, dispatch document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('chat-scroll-to', { detail: { messageId } })) for the located message id, set userToggledRef.current = true to suppress auto-collapse, add role='button', tabIndex={0}, Enter/Space key handlers. No-op silently when no match — do NOT dispatch the event.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an agent card scrolls the chat to the matching tool call
Cards have keyboard focus support (Tab and Enter work)
Clicking a card with no matching message does not crash, scroll, or close the panel (failure case)

tasks.title: Rust test — scan_skills extracts description and body excerpt
tasks.description: Extend the tests module in src-tauri/src/commands/claude_config.rs starting at line 590. Add scan_skills_extracts_description_from_frontmatter (writes fake SKILL.md, asserts Some('Foo bar')), scan_skills_returns_none_when_description_missing, scan_skills_body_excerpt_truncates_long_body (asserts <= ~200 chars), scan_skills_body_excerpt_skips_headings. Use existing write_file helper at line 594 and tempfile pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: All four new test cases pass under bun run test:rust
Tests use the existing write_file helper and tempfile pattern
A SKILL.md with a single-character description (edge case) still parses correctly (failure case: 'description: x' yields Some('x'))

tasks.title: Test skillsPaletteStore state transitions
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/stores/skillsPaletteStore.test.ts. Cases: open sets isOpen=true; close resets isOpen/query/selectedIndex; setQuery resets selectedIndex to 0; moveSelection clamps within bounds; bail-out guard verified via subscribe spy that open() called twice does not trigger a second state change. Follow patterns in settingsStore.test.ts and diffStore.test.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: 100% coverage of all actions exported by the store
At least one test verifies the bail-out guard does not double-fire set
Test runs under bun run test (Vitest), not bun test (failure case if mistakenly run with Bun's native runner per bunfig.toml redirect)

tasks.title: Component test — SkillsPalette rendering, filtering, navigation, invocation
tasks.description: New file src/renderer/components/chat/SkillsPalette.test.tsx. Cases: renders when isOpen=true and nothing when false; fuzzy filter ranks 'comm' surfacing commit ahead of cost-estimate; ArrowDown moves selection and clamps; Enter calls invocation handler once; Escape calls close; empty state renders 'No skills match'; Cmd+K keydown opens palette. Use @testing-library/react, mock useClaudeConfigStore with skills fixture, mock useSkillInvoke return spy.
acceptanceCriteria: All seven cases above pass
Mocking useSkillInvoke returns a spy that is called exactly once on Enter
Skills with description: undefined still render without throwing (failure case)

tasks.title: Component test — ContextRing tooltip extension
tasks.description: Extend src/renderer/components/chat/ContextRing.test.tsx. Add cases: tooltip shows breakdown rows when all four token fields present; falls back to single-line summary when breakdown missing; auto-compact estimate renders 'soon' at pct >= 95; cost row displays in $X.XX format using formatCost. Original tests at line 8 are the baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: All four new assertions pass alongside existing tests
When passed used=0, size=100, tooltip shows 0% and an estimate > 0 turns until auto-compact (failure case: must not show 'in -1 turns')
Cost formatting renders correctly for both penny-scale and dollar-scale values

tasks.title: Component test — AcpSubagentDisplay nested tree, role colors, click-to-focus
tasks.description: Extend src/renderer/components/chat/AcpSubagentDisplay.test.tsx. Cases: subagents with parent='agent-a' render indented under agent-a; two siblings render at same indent; circular parent references render flat without infinite loop; click on agent card dispatches chat-scroll-to or calls scroll helper with correct id; role-colored icons differ between plan and research; elapsed clock element appears only when running. Use SubagentInfo[] fixture; mock document.dispatchEvent. Use vi.useFakeTimers for elapsed clock.
acceptanceCriteria: All six new assertions pass alongside existing tests
Existing flat-list tests still pass (regression preserved)
Test for circular reference asserts within ~3 second timeout — no recursion stack overflow (failure case)

tasks.title: Update docs/keyboard-shortcuts.md with Cmd+K Skills Palette entry
tasks.description: Append a new section to docs/keyboard-shortcuts.md: Cmd+K (macOS) / Ctrl+K (Linux/Windows) 'Open Skills Palette'. Inside palette: up/down navigate, enter invoke, esc close, type to fuzzy-filter. One-line note that skills come from ~/.claude/skills/ and .claude/skills/. Keep existing structure unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: New section is appended in the existing style (matching headers and table format)
Mentions both macOS and non-macOS modifiers
Existing shortcut documentation is unchanged byte-for-byte (failure case: diff must be additive only)

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md engineering learnings + append activity.md entry
tasks.description: Add two bullets to ## Engineering learnings in CLAUDE.md: (1) Skills palette pattern — reuse splash-insert CustomEvent + fuzzy-search + Radix Dialog; always close palette via getState().close() from invoke handler not modal onOpenChange to avoid stale state on side-effect invocations. (2) Subagent nested tree — detect circular parent references before rendering; elapsed-time clocks tick from first running transition only. Then prepend a new entry to activity.md in existing format (Dubai GST timestamp, title, 1-3 sentence summary, **Modified:** line). Entry summarizes entire Claude Code Superpowers batch.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md has both new learning bullets in the Engineering learnings section
activity.md gets one new entry prepended (not appended)
Activity log entry timestamps use Dubai GST and follow the existing YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM GST (Dubai) format (failure case: any other timezone or format breaks the convention)
